name: Dehesilla de Guadalcázar and Vía Verde de la Campiña Loop
name_original: Ruta de la Dehesilla de Guadalcázar — circular por la Vía Verde de la Campiña
description: Hike the moderate 9.2-mile Dehesilla de Guadalcázar and Vía Verde de la Campiña Loop through diverse Cordoban campiña, olive groves, and his
region_intro: Hiking around Guadalcázar offers diverse landscapes, situated on the western edge of the Sierra Gorda mountains in San Luis Potosí, Mexico. The region features a blend of geological wonders, including limestone caves and granite formations, alongside rolling hills, fields, and olive groves. Its terrain also borders the Chihuahuan Desert, adding distinct ecological dimensions to the hiking experience. Best hiking trails around Guadalcázar The most popular hiking route is Embalse de la Breña - circular from Almodóvar del Río, a 7.2 miles (11.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 12 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Dehesilla de Guadalcázar and Vía Verde de la Campiña Loop, a moderate 9.2 miles (14.8 km) path. This route winds through diverse Cordoban campiña and olive groves. Local hikers also love the Carolinas Fountains Trail, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through accessible paths, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Hiking around Guadalcázar is defined by limestone caves, granite formations, and diverse terrain including rolling hills and olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Guadalcázar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Guadalcázar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Guadalcázar?
A: Hiking in Guadalcázar offers a diverse landscape, from the rugged Sierra Gorda mountains to rolling hills, fields, and olive groves. You'll encounter unique geological features like limestone caves, impressive granite formations, and areas bordering the Chihuahuan Desert, providing a rich variety of scenery.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Guadalcázar has several easy hiking options. There are 9 easy routes available, perfect for beginners or families. One accessible path is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248228530/carolinas-fountains-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carolinas Fountains Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 6.5 km long and takes around 1 hour 44 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Guadalcázar?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in the area. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248122664/dehesilla-de-guadalcazar-and-via-verde-de-la-campina-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesilla de Guadalcázar and Vía Verde de la Campiña Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 14.8 km trail that winds through diverse Cordoban campiña and olive groves.

Q: What are some natural landmarks or points of interest I can see while hiking?
A: Guadalcázar is rich in natural landmarks. You can explore impressive limestone caves like Grutas de San Cayetano and Grutas Las Candelas, known for their stalactites and tufas. Other features include large granite formations at 'Cerro de las Comadres' and the distinctive 'Los Arcos' rock formation. Some trails also lead through beautiful pine areas and past lakes, such as those found near El Realejo.

Q: Are there any historical sites or ruins to discover on the trails?
A: Absolutely. Many hiking paths in Guadalcázar incorporate historical elements. You can discover sections of the old 'Camino Real,' once used for mineral transport, along with ancient constructions, ruined houses, wells, and abandoned mines like Mina de la Galana and Mina de la Luz, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Guadalcázar?
A: The hiking routes in Guadalcázar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the unique geological features like caves and granite formations, and the variety of trails suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of castles or historical buildings?
A: Yes, some routes provide excellent views of historical structures. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24522994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Almodóvar del Río – Almodóvar del Río Castle loop from Almodóvar del Río&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic vistas of the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/384616" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Almodóvar del Río&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5408791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mocha Tower of the Palace of the Marquises of Guadalcázar&lt;/a&gt; along some paths.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to significant archaeological sites?
A: Yes, some routes are close to important historical areas. You can find trails that lead near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/632043" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medina Azahara Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, offering a chance to explore this significant historical landmark.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Guadalcázar?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations can vary, the diverse terrain of Guadalcázar, with its blend of mountainous areas and desert borders, suggests that spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for hiking. Summers can be hot, especially in areas bordering the Chihuahuan Desert, while winters are typically mild.

Q: Are there any trails that feature caves or opportunities for cave exploration?
A: Absolutely. The region is renowned for its limestone caves. The hike to Grutas de San Cayetano is a popular choice, offering a pleasant one-hour walk to explore its depths. Grutas Las Candelas also provides excellent hiking opportunities through a state reserve to reach its impressive cave system. The Zelda Cave is another option for exploration.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Guadalcázar?
A: Guadalcázar offers a good selection of hiking trails, with 39 routes available for exploration. These range from easy paths to more challenging routes, catering to various fitness levels.
tips: Modest town in the Cordoba countryside whose silhouette is distinguished thanks to the Mocha Tower, from which in turn you have a good view of the surroundings. Near the town passes the Vía Verde de la Campiña, which offers a pleasant walk along an old abandoned train track.
Rest area of the Vía verde de la Campiña. It has picnic tables and several trees for lounging in the shade.

The path is part of the Vía Verde de la Campiña. A beautiful setting between fields and trees, perfect for walks or cycling. It has several rest areas. Along the way we can enjoy the typical landscapes of this part of the province of Córdoba.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Dehesilla de Guadalcázar and Vía Verde de la Campiña Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop is approximately 14.8 km (9.2 miles) long and generally takes around 4 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the Vía Verde de la Campiña section is mostly flat and accessible, the overall loop is rated as moderate. It involves some varied terrain, including a descent to the Arroyo de la Marota and an ascent to the Altos del Baldío. Hikers with good physical condition will find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter sections of 'vía pecuaria' (livestock trail) with Mediterranean scrubland, followed by the Vía Verde de la Campiña which features a mix of asphalt and compacted earth. The route also passes through cereal fields, traditional olive groves, and unique holm oak dehesas.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the loop?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the historic Torre Mocha, Guadalcázar, the La Pineda rest area, and the scenic Campiña landscape. You'll also see remnants of the old railway line, such as the Las Tablas tunnel and bridges over streams, as well as the former Guadalcázar railway station, now a recreational area.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Dehesilla area is an 'island of biodiversity' with unique relict vegetation like holm oaks and wild olives. Birdwatchers might spot species such as the crested lark, bustard, stone curlew, and even endangered species like the alcaraván and sisón común.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring is an excellent time to visit, as the fields of sunflowers and olive groves are particularly vibrant. The weather is generally pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn.

faqs.question: Where can I park and start the Dehesilla de Guadalcázar and Vía Verde de la Campiña Loop?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking and begin the loop near the town of Guadalcázar. The former Guadalcázar railway station, now a recreational area, is a good starting point with amenities.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Dehesilla de Guadalcázar and Vía Verde de la Campiña Loop.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails, including the Camino Natural Vía Verde de la Campiña II, Vía Verde de la Campiña I (Valchillón - Écija), and other parts of the Via Verde de la Campiña.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or rest areas along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find the La Pineda rest area along the route. The former Guadalcázar railway station has also been converted into a recreational area with amenities, and Parque de El Hecho offers a pine forest with picnic facilities.
